headlines.text: Trump says he won't 'rush into' Iran deal as GOP senators warn it could be a 'disastrous mistake'
Gunman opens fire near White House checkpoint, killed by Secret Service — third incident in a month
ABC reporter Selina Wang's viral video of diving for cover during White House shooting viewed 3M+ times
Rubio says 'significant progress' made on Iran peace framework, Strait of Hormuz partially reopening
Memorial Day gas prices hit four-year high at $4.56/gal as Iran war keeps Strait of Hormuz choked
Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu opens to $102M over Memorial Day — worst Star Wars debut since Solo
Dow hits intraday all-time high as stocks post eighth straight winning week on Middle East peace hopes
NYT publisher A.G. Sulzberger blasts Trump press attacks at Yale Law School, accuses outlets of caving
Trump admin overhauls green card policy — foreigners in US must now leave and apply from home country
SpaceX IPO confirmed, targeting $1.75 trillion Nasdaq valuation with debut expected June 12

breaking.text: THIRD SHOOTING NEAR WHITE HOUSE IN A MONTH: Gunman Nasire Best, 21, opens fire at Secret Service checkpoint on Pennsylvania Ave, killed by officers. Trump was inside.

stories.hed: Deal Is 'Largely Negotiated' — Subject to Finalization, Two Sides Agreeing, Iran's Supreme Leader, Congress, and Gravity Itself
stories.dek: Trump went from '50/50 chance of blowing them to kingdom come' on Saturday to 'time is on our side, no rush' on Sunday — a diplomatic whiplash measured in hours. Rubio, calling in from India, says there's been 'significant progress,' while GOP Sen. Tillis says the deal is 'doomed to fail' and Israel is privately furious it signals the Strait is Iran's new nuclear weapon.

stories.hed: Iran's Supreme Leader Orders Enriched Uranium Stays Inside Iran, Complicating Core US Demand
stories.dek: Tehran's Supreme Leader is drawing a hard red line just as both sides were getting cozy over deal terms — insisting enriched uranium won't leave the country. That happens to be a central US requirement, so the 60-day nuclear negotiation window in the proposed framework is starting to look more like a 60-day countdown to something else.

stories.hed: Trump Administration Ends In-Country Green Card Renewals — Immigrants Must Return Home to Apply
stories.dek: In a Friday-afternoon policy grenade, the Trump administration announced that foreigners seeking green cards must leave the US and apply from their home countries, reversing a longstanding process that let people adjust status domestically. Millions of people who built lives here are now staring at a very inconvenient boarding pass.

stories.hed: Third Shooting Near White House in a Month Raises Alarming Security Questions
stories.dek: A gunman opened fire at a Secret Service checkpoint at 17th St and Pennsylvania Ave on Saturday, wounding a bystander before being fatally shot — the third such incident in 30 days following the White House Correspondents' Dinner assassination attempt and a May 4 shooting near the Washington Monument. At this rate, Trump's security detail is putting in more overtime than his social media team.

stories.hed: NYT Publisher Calls Out Media Outlets for Rolling Over — But Who Is He Really Talking To?
stories.dek: A.G. Sulzberger, speaking at Yale Law School, dropped a thinly veiled critique of outlets that settled Trump lawsuits, softened editorial stances, or adopted administration-friendly language to protect business interests. The speech was principled, the timing was pointed, and everyone in the room knew exactly which logos he was picturing.

markets.context: Stocks hit eight straight winning weeks as Middle East peace optimism lifted sentiment, even as oil fell on hopes the Strait of Hormuz could reopen under a US-Iran deal.

gas.note: Filling your tank now costs more than a therapist copay — and unlike therapy, it provides zero peace of mind.

popCulture.hed: Star Wars Returns: The Mandalorian and Grogu Opens to $102M Over Memorial Day — But Is It Enough?
popCulture.dek: The first Star Wars film in seven years blasted off with a $102M four-day Memorial Day haul, earning an A- CinemaScore with families and men over 55 — but analysts are mixed since it technically trails Solo: A Star Wars Story. Baby Yoda has 13 million toys sold and all the goodwill in the galaxy, which is doing more heavy lifting than the box office right now.

genZ.definition: ABC News correspondent Selina Wang's video of herself mid-iPhone selfie-report suddenly dropping to the ground as gunshots erupted outside the White House went viral with over 3 million views in hours. The clip captured the exact moment normal press-pool chaos became actual chaos, and the internet turned it into everything from memes to genuine terror.
genZ.usage: When Trump announced the Iran deal was 'largely negotiated subject to finalization' it felt like the Selina Wang video — everything is fine, everything is fine, then the floor drops out.

tldr: Trump spent Memorial Day weekend simultaneously announcing an Iran peace deal is 'largely done' and saying there's 'no rush' to finish it, while a gunman was shot dead outside the White House for the third time in a month. Gas hit a four-year high at $4.56, the Dow hit an all-time high, Baby Yoda opened to $102M, and everyone online watched a reporter dive for cover — so, a perfectly normal American Sunday.
